quality.rationale: 基于宇宙学共动坐标系物理意义的证明题，逻辑链条清晰且具有真实天体运动背景

origin.answer: 沿星系世界线（$x^i = \\text{const}$）有 $\\mathrm{d}x^i = 0$，代入度规得 $\\mathrm{d}s^2 = -\\mathrm{d}\\tau^2$，故 $\\tau$ 为固有时。
origin.solution:
# Solution 19.38.
(a) A galaxy moves with $\mathbf{x}^{\mathbf{i}} =$ constant implying that $\mathrm{dx}^{\mathrm{i}} = 0$ and hence that $\mathrm{ds}^2 = -\mathrm{dr}^2$ .
(b) For $\mathrm{d}\tau = 0$ , $\mathrm{ds}^2 = \mathbf{g}_{ij} \mathrm{dx}^i \mathrm{dx}^j$ .
(c) If $\mathbf{g}_{0i}$ and $\mathbf{g}_{ij}$ are independent of $\mathbf{x}^i$ , then on constant $r$ hypersurfaces, nothing depends upon $\mathbf{x}^i$ ; the space is homogeneous. On the other hand, a bad choice of coordinates and hence a seemingly distorted spacelike hypersurface may cause the metric functions of a homogeneous space to depend upon position.
(d) In Problem 14.9 we showed that if the spatial connecting vector between any two nearby geodesics is of constant length, then $\sigma_{\alpha \beta} = \theta = 0$ for the geodesic field. If $g_{0i}$ and $g_{ij}$ are independent of $	au$ , then the connecting vector will have constant length.
(e) Since $\mathbf{u}^0 = +1$ , $\mathbf{u}^i = 0$ , we have
$$
\begin{array}{l}
 u_{i ; j} = - u^{\gamma} \Gamma_{\gamma i j} = - \Gamma_{0 i j} = - \frac {1} {2} \left(g_{i 0 , j} + g_{0 j , i} - g_{i j , 0} \right) \tag {1} \\
 = \frac {1} {2} g_{i j , 0} = \frac {1} {2} f \bar {g}_{i j} . \\
 \end{array}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{l}
 \theta \equiv u^{a} ; a = | g |^{- \frac {1} {2}} \left(| g |^{\frac {1} {2}} u^{a} \right) , a = f^{- \frac {3} {2}} \left( f^{\frac {3} {2}} \right) , 0 \tag {2} \\
 = \frac {3} {2} \frac {\mathrm {f}} {\mathrm {f}} . \\
 \end{array}
$$
Now, from Equations (1), (2), and the formula (see Problem 5.18)
$$
\sigma_{\alpha ; \beta} = \frac {1} {2} \left( \mathrm {u}_{\alpha ; \mu} \mathrm {P}^{\mu}_{\beta} + \mathrm {u}_{\beta ; \mu} \mathrm {P}^{\mu}_{\alpha} \right) - \frac {1} {3} \theta \mathrm {P}_{\alpha} \beta \tag {3}
$$
we arrive at
$$
\sigma_{i j} = \frac {1} {2} \left( u_{i ; j} + u_{j ; i} \right) - \frac {1} {3} \theta g_{i j} = \frac {1} {2} \dot {f} \bar {g}_{i j} - \frac {1} {2} \dot {f} \bar {g}_{i j} = 0 . \tag {4}
$$
$$
a^{\alpha} = u^{\alpha} ; \beta u^{\beta} = u^{\alpha} ; 0 = \Gamma^{\alpha}_{0 0} \tag {f}
$$
$$
\mathrm {a}_{\alpha} = \Gamma_{\alpha 0 0} = \frac {1} {2} \left( \mathrm {g}_{\alpha 0 , 0} + \mathrm {g}_{\alpha 0 , 0} - \mathrm {g}_{0 0 , \alpha} \right) = \mathrm {g}_{0 \alpha , 0} .
$$
If $\mathbf{a}_{\alpha} = 0$ , then $\mathbf{g}_{0\alpha,0} = 0$ and galaxies fall on geodesics if and only if $\mathbf{g}_{0\mathbf{i},0} = 0$ .
(g) From Problem 7.23 we showed that the necessary condition for a family of hypersurfaces to be orthogonal to $\mathbf{u}$ is $\omega_{\alpha \beta} = 0$ . Since $\mathbf{u} = \partial/\partial \tau$ and the hypersurfaces of constant $	au$ are spanned by $\partial/\partial \mathbf{x}^{\mathbf{i}}$ , then $\omega_{\alpha \beta} \neq 0$ implies that $(\partial/\partial t) \cdot (\partial/\partial \mathbf{x}^{\mathbf{i}}) \neq 0$ and hence that $\mathbf{g}_{0\mathbf{i}} \neq 0$ . If $\omega_{\alpha \beta,0} \neq 0$ , then $\mathbf{g}_{\alpha \beta,0} \neq 0$ and from part (f), this means that the galaxies do not fall on geodesics.
